<!-- GENERATED by scripts/agent-rules/generate.mjs — edit that, not this file. -->

Resolve Auto Edit (brief → render) work. Apply when: the user names source files, optional music, and the kind of video they want and expects a finished cut — autonomous talking-head/interview editing with one approval checkpoint.

Two servers — compute offline, apply live:
- Live (running Resolve, `davinci-resolve`): auto_edit (start_brief, brief_status, plan_cut, revise_cut, get_cut_summary, approve_cut, build_timeline, finish), media_analysis, edit_engine
- Offline (.drp/.drt/.drx + DB, no Resolve, `davinci-resolve-advanced`): cut_ir/auto_edit decision layer (pure planning against the analysis DB); Phase 2 adds drt surgery via the advanced server
- Key rule: ONE human checkpoint: approve_cut (confirm-token gated; its preview carries the cut summary + the music-bed-render consent line). Timelines are stateless artifacts — revisions rebuild via append-rebuild, never hand-patch. The only permitted derivative is the consent-gated ducked music bed, written under the analysis root.

Depth: docs/kernels/auto-edit-kernel.md + docs/guides/editorial-decision-guide.md. On-demand in any MCP client: the `/auto_edit_workflow` slash prompt. Claude Code skill: `resolve-auto-edit`. Never modify/transcode/derive source media (AGENTS.md).
